name: Christmas Vacation
description: National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation (1989) — cast, characters, and production trivia.

questions.q: Who plays Clark Griswold in National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation?

source.notes: Clark Griswold is the lead; Chevy Chase played him in all Vacation films.

questions.q: In what year was National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation released?

source.notes: Released December 1, 1989.

questions.q: Which actor plays the memorable Cousin Eddie?

source.notes: Cousin Eddie, played by Randy Quaid in multiple Vacation entries.

questions.q: What is the name of Cousin Eddie's dog?

source.notes: Eddie's mutt Snots is a recurring sight gag.

questions.q: What underwhelming gift does Clark receive from his boss instead of a holiday bonus?

source.original_a: Jelly of the Month club membership
source.notes: Eddie's summary: 'the gift that keeps on giving.'

questions.q: In the film, Clark famously struggles to get what kind of Christmas display to work?

source.notes: The massive 25,000-bulb house-lighting gag.

questions.q: Which holiday does the film center on?

source.notes: Title-evident.

questions.q: Which actress plays Clark's wife, Ellen Griswold?

source.notes: D'Angelo reprises Ellen from Vacation (1983).

questions.q: Who plays Clark's teenage son Rusty in Christmas Vacation?

source.notes: Galecki later starred in The Big Bang Theory.

questions.q: Who plays Audrey Griswold, Clark's daughter, in this film?

source.notes: Rusty and Audrey are recast each film.

questions.q: Which actor plays Clark's stingy boss, Frank Shirley?

source.notes: Brian Doyle-Murray — Bill Murray's older brother.

questions.q: Which future Seinfeld star plays yuppie neighbor Margo?

source.notes: Pre-Elaine-era role.

questions.q: Christmas Vacation is based on a John Hughes short story published in National Lampoon. What was it called?

source.notes: Hughes's companion piece to 'Vacation '58', the basis of the original film.

questions.q: Who directed National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation?

source.notes: Chechik's feature debut; Chris Columbus was originally attached.

questions.q: Which patriotic song does Aunt Bethany recite when asked to say grace at the dinner?

source.notes: Mae Questel as confused Aunt Bethany (she also voiced Betty Boop).

questions.q: Which Christmas Vacation character ends up kidnapping Clark's boss?

source.notes: Eddie delivers Frank Shirley to Clark as a 'gift.'

questions.q: Which timeless Christmas film is playing on TV as the extended Griswold family gathers?

source.notes: Black-and-white clip plays during family scene.

questions.q: Which actor plays Uncle Lewis, who memorably sets the Christmas tree on fire?

source.notes: Hickey: Oscar-nominated for Prizzi's Honor.

questions.q: Which 1988 sci-fi/horror film features the same junky Dodge pickup that tailgates Clark in the opening scene?

source.notes: John Carpenter's They Live (1988) — same production vehicle.

questions.q: After dropping out of directing Christmas Vacation, Chris Columbus went on to direct which smash holiday comedy?

source.notes: Home Alone (1990), also a John Hughes project.

questions.q: Who performed the title song 'Christmas Vacation' over the opening credits?

source.notes: Penned for the film.

questions.q: Who composed the score for National Lampoon's Christmas Vacation?

source.notes: Best known for Twin Peaks.
